segments.text: As he went along, he saw a man blind from birth.
His disciples asked him,
Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: said Jesus,
Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but this happened so that the works of God might be displayed in him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As long as it is day, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming, when no one can work.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After saying this, he spit on the ground, made some mud with the saliva, and put it on the man's eyes.
he told him,
Go, wash in the Pool of Siloam
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: (this word means "Sent"). So the man went and washed, and came home seeing.
His neighbors and those who had formerly seen him begging asked,
Isn't this the same man who used to sit and beg?
segments.speaker: Neighbors

segments.text: Some claimed that he was. Others said,
No, he only looks like him.
segments.speaker: Some People

segments.text: But he himself insisted,
I am the man.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: they asked,
How then were your eyes opened?
segments.speaker: Neighbors

segments.text: He replied,
The man they call Jesus made some mud and put it on my eyes. He told me to go to Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: they asked him.
Where is this man?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: I don't know.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had been blind.
Now the day on which Jesus had made the mud and opened the man's eyes was a Sabbath.
Therefore the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight.
He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and now I see.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Some of the Pharisees said,
This man is not from God, for he does not keep the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: Some Pharisees

segments.text: But others asked,
How can a sinner perform such signs?
segments.speaker: Other Pharisees

segments.text: So they were divided.
Then they turned again to the blind man,
What have you to say about him? It was your eyes he opened.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: The man replied,
He is a prophet.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: They still did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight until they sent for the man's parents.
they asked,
Is this your son? Is this the one you say was born blind? How is it that now he can see?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: the parents answered,
We know he is our son, and we know he was born blind.
segments.speaker: Parents

segments.text: But how he can see now, or who opened his eyes, we don't know. Ask him. He is of age; he will speak for himself.
segments.speaker: Parents

segments.text: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jewish leaders, who already had decided that anyone who acknowledged that Jesus was the Messiah would be put out of the synagogue.
That was why his parents said,
He is of age; ask him.
segments.speaker: Parents

segments.text: A second time they summoned the man who had been blind.
Give glory to God by telling the truth. We know this man is a sinner.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: He replied,
Whether he is a sinner or not, I don't know. One thing I do know. I was blind but now I see!
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Then they asked him,
What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: He answered,
I have told you already and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too?
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Then they hurled insults at him and said,
You are this fellow's disciple! We are disciples of Moses!
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: We know that God spoke to Moses, but as for this fellow, we don't even know where he comes from.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: The man answered,
Now that is remarkable! You don't know where he comes from, yet he opened my eyes.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: We know that God does not listen to sinners. He listens to the godly person who does his will.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Nobody has ever heard of opening the eyes of a man born blind.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: To this they replied,
You were steeped in sin at birth; how dare you lecture us!
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: And they threw him out.
Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and when he found him, he said,
Do you believe in the Son of Man?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: the man asked,
Who is he, sir? Tell me so that I may believe in him.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: You have now seen him; in fact, he is the one speaking with you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the man said,
Lord, I believe
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: and he worshiped him.
For judgment I have come into this world, so that the blind will see and those who see will become blind.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Some Pharisees who were with him heard him say this and asked,
What? Are we blind too?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: If you were blind, you would not be guilty of sin; but now that you claim you can see, your guilt remains.
segments.speaker: Jesus
